subject: Biology
prompt: Explain how photosynthesis and cellular respiration are related.
reference_answer: Photosynthesis stores energy by converting carbon dioxide and water into glucose using sunlight. Cellular respiration releases that stored energy by breaking down glucose into ATP. The products of one process are the reactants of the other.

student_answers.response: Photosynthesis converts sunlight into chemical energy stored in glucose, while cellular respiration releases that energy as ATP. Together they cycle matter and energy.
Plants make glucose using sunlight and cells use glucose to make energy.
Photosynthesis makes food and respiration gives energy.
Photosynthesis and respiration are exactly the same process.
Plants only breathe oxygen and never use carbon dioxide.
